name: Notta
description: Multilingual transcription and meeting notes app, web + mobile

developer.comment: Notta is developed by Langogo Technology; the team is associated with offices in Tokyo and Shenzhen.

first-release.comment: Notta's transcription app launched around 2020, but no authoritative month-precise first-release date is documented; left null per the murky-history guideline rather than guessing a month.

status.comment: Actively developed and sold as of June 2026.

license.comment: Proprietary SaaS with a usable free tier; freemium is the closest license tag.

primary-type.comment: Core job is recording/file transcription plus a meeting bot that joins calls and produces AI notes/summaries.

real-time-dictation.comment: Notta transcribes live audio/meetings in real time but does not offer system-wide speak-to-type dictation as a productivity input method.

file-transcription.comment: Supports uploading audio (MP3, WAV, M4A, etc.) and video files for transcription.

live-meeting-capture.comment: A meeting bot auto-joins Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, and Webex calls.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Produces a live running transcript of ongoing recordings/meetings in real time.

audio-sources.comment: Microphone/device recording, file upload, and a meeting bot. No documented arbitrary URL import.

underlying-engine.comment: Notta states it uses the optimal engine per language; AmiVoice (Advanced Media) powers Japanese, but the full engine stack is not disclosed, so no single engine is recorded.

language-count.comment: Vendor states 58 transcription languages (approx; some marketing materials cite 104+ counting translation/regional variants). Checked June 2026.

english-accuracy.comment: Vendor claims 98%+ accuracy but provides no independent WER. Reviews report solid results on clear English while accuracy and diarization drop on accents/overlapping speech — anchored to 3 (solid, error-prone on hard audio).

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Supports multilingual transcription including bilingual and real-time translation modes.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Custom vocabulary on paid plans (up to 200 entries on Pro, more on Business); limited to English and Japanese.

speaker-diarization.comment: Automatic speaker identification (labels up to ~8-10 speakers); accuracy degrades with overlapping speech and accents.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Auto-generates summaries, key points, and action items after sessions.

built-in-editor.comment: In-app transcript editor for reviewing and correcting text (plain text correction, not media-by-text editing).

translation.comment: Translates transcripts into ~42 languages; real-time and bilingual translation modes available.

export-formats.comment: TXT, DOCX, PDF, SRT (plus XLSX, not in the tag set). Export is a paid-plan feature, unavailable on the free tier.

processing-location.comment: Audio is processed in the cloud; no on-device/local processing option.

data-used-for-training.comment: Per reviews of the privacy posture, paid-tier data is not used for training; free/individual-account content may be used in de-identified form by default with opt-out via support. Notta's primary policy page is JS-rendered and could not be fetched directly; treat the consent posture as approximate.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can delete individual transcripts/recordings or their account; retention/auto-deletion configurable on higher tiers.

platforms.comment: Web app, iOS, Android, Chrome extension, and a Mac App Store app; Windows is supported via the browser rather than a native desktop app.

system-wide-dictation.comment: No OS-wide speak-to-type; functionality is confined to the Notta apps.

integrations.comment: Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, Webex (no Webex tag), Slack, Notion, Google Calendar, plus CRMs like Salesforce/HubSpot and Zapier.

api-automation.comment: Offers API access and Zapier integration for custom automation workflows.

pricing-model.comment: Freemium with paid subscription tiers (Pro, Business, Enterprise).

free-tier.comment: Genuine free plan, not just a trial.

free-allowance.value: 120 min/mo (3-5 min per recording), no export
free-allowance.comment: Free plan: 120 transcription minutes/month with a short per-recording cap and limited AI summaries; export is paid-only. Checked June 2026.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro plan ~$8.17/mo billed annually (~$13.49/mo billed monthly) per seat. Checked June 2026.
